# n1 — tdd-guide evidence (issue #351)

## RED
Against the unmodified hook: (a) `git -C <repo> commit` with cross-project staging → exit 0 (BUG, expected 2 — ASSERTION FAILED captured); (b) `git -c user.name=Bot commit` → exit 0 (BUG, ASSERTION FAILED captured); (c) plain `git commit` → exit 2 (correct). RED confirmed for the two flag-bearing forms.

## GREEN
After the fix, all five scenario assertions pass:
(a) git -C <repo> commit, cross-project in TARGET repo → exit 2 BLOCKED "split your commit — multiple kaola-workflow projects staged: alpha beta"
(b) git -c k=v commit, cross-project → exit 2 BLOCKED
(c) plain git commit → exit 2 BLOCKED (regression preserved)
(d) git -C <repo> status → exit 0 untouched
(e) git -C <repo> commit, single-project → exit 0 allowed
ALL GREEN.

## Fix
Fast-path `*"git commit"*` substring case preserved (compound `cd x && git commit` detection unregressed); added a tokenizer (node -e, single-quoted) that skips git global flags (-C <path>, -c <kv>, --long[=val]) before the subcommand and reports `commit` + the extracted -C path; when -C present, INSPECT_ROOT=<path> so `git -C "$INSPECT_ROOT" diff --cached` inspects the RIGHT repo. Fail-open posture preserved (parse error / non-commit → exit 0).

## Byte-identity ×4 (shasum, all identical)
0d6f09e2487302dd4d3915c94ecddb3a4e344150 for hooks/kaola-workflow-pre-commit.sh + the 3 plugin copies.

## Verification
New walkthrough scenario testHookGitDashCCommitGuard registered next to testHookSingleProjectGuard: PASSED in the full run; `node scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js` → "Workflow walkthrough simulation passed" exit 0; `node scripts/validate-script-sync.js` → "OK: 18 common scripts and 7 byte-identical file group in sync" exit 0.

Files written: exactly the 5 declared (walkthrough + 4 hook copies).
